questions.question: In paragraph 8, Teacher Zhang mentions that Ji-li “participates in educational reform”. What inference can be made about his motivation for bringing this up?

responses.content: That they were gonna expose her family in front of her teachers, and classmates

questions.question:
Read the following passage from paragraph 10:

“I closed my eyes. I saw Dad standing on a stage, his head bowed, his name written in large black letters, and then crossed out in red ink, on a sign hanging from his neck. I saw myself standing in the middle of the stage, facing thousands of people, condemning Dad for his crimes, raising my fist to lead the chant, ‘Down with Jiang Xi-reng.’ I saw Dad looking at me hopelessly, tears on his face.”

What does this suggest about Ji-li's relationship with her father?

responses.content: Because jiang ji-li has always done qel
Because jiang ji-li has always done well in school and doing well in her studies that’s why he asked her to participate in educational reform.

questions.question: Which of the following inferences about the interrogation is best supported by the text?

responses.content: Because she felt a small animal that had fallen into a trap, alone and helpless which made her into a emotional state

questions.question: Which of the following best expresses a central idea of the excerpt?

responses.content: B. While government officials can be intimidating, children in China know that they can rely on their teachers to support them.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text most strongly supports the answer to the previous question?

responses.content: D. “If you want to make a clean break with your black family, then you can be an educable child and we will welcome you to our revolutionary ranks.”

activities.text_title: Heroes Every Child Should Know: Perseus

questions.question: Look at paragraphs 46-47. How do Perseus's actions show that he followed Athene's plan?

responses.content: He wrapped the head in a goat skin turning his eyes away and after ran away

questions.question: What does Perseus's response to the Gorgons in paragraph 49 reveal about his state of mind?

responses.content: Perseus struck her with the sword making her fall and dying never needing to struck again
So he didn’t turn to stone

questions.question: In paragraph 49, Perseus calls the sandals “brave.” What does this reveal about Perseus' character in this moment?
Based on Perseus's actions in paragraphs 46 through 47, the reader can conclude that—

responses.content: A. Athene will be pleased with the resolution of the plot's conflict.

questions.question: When Perseus's blood runs cold in paragraph 49, it reveals that he has—

responses.content: C. brief doubt in Athene's plan.
